body:
Morning,

Following up on ticket #48213 from last week about the tide predictions on the app lagging for the Thomas Point station. It's still showing high tide close to an hour off from what I'm actually seeing off the stern before first light. I run pots out of Annapolis on the Bay Runner and that hour is the difference between setting gear on a good flood or fighting slack water for nothing.

Also the marine forecast widget for Chesapeake Bay central still isn't updating wind shift data past 10am - just freezes on whatever it grabbed overnight. I need that number before I commit to a charter run or decide if it's a pots-only day.

Would appreciate someone actually looking at the backend on this rather than the reinstall-the-app line I got last time. I've got charters booked this weekend and need to trust the numbers.

Thanks,
Jack Singleton
F/V Bay Runner, Annapolis

Hi Jack,

Thanks for the follow-up, and sorry for the wait. I pulled #48213 - you were right, it wasn't a fluke. The Thomas Point tide feed was pulling from a cached NOAA source that hadn't refreshed since Sunday night. Engineering pushed a fix yesterday that forces a refresh every 15 minutes instead of once a day, so predictions should track a lot closer to real time now.

The wind shift freeze is a separate issue tied to how the app handles the 10am NOAA model update specifically for the Chesapeake Bay central zone - a couple other captains in your area flagged the same thing. There's a patch scheduled for next week's release. Until then, force-closing and reopening the app after 10am should trigger a manual pull and get you current data.

Could you check the tide numbers tomorrow morning before you head out and let me know if they line up with what you're seeing at the marina? I'd rather have you confirm it than assume it's fixed on our end, especially with charters this weekend.

Best,
Siobhan

Hey Siobhan,

Following up on the ticket from last week about the AM medication reminder -- the 7:15 alert is still popping before I've even had coffee, let alone breakfast. Most mornings I'm up around 5:30 to check the marine forecast and tide charts for the central Bay before I head out to the marina, so by the time the reminder hits I'm already halfway out the door with a to-go mug and the pills sit on the counter till I'm back at noon.

Can you push the AM dose reminder to 6:00 instead, and maybe add a second nudge around 6:30 in case I miss the first one? I take the Losartan 50mg and the Gabapentin 300mg together with breakfast, so timing actually matters to me here -- don't want to be doubling up or skipping by accident with the boat schedule the way it is.

Appreciate you sorting this out.

Jack Singleton

Good morning Mr. Singleton,

Thanks for the detail -- that helps a lot. I've updated your AM reminder window to 6:00 with a follow-up nudge at 6:30 if the first one isn't marked as taken. Both the Losartan and Gabapentin are still grouped together under one "AM with breakfast" alert like you had it, so you'll get a single prompt rather than two separate ones.

One thing I want to flag: our records show the app's default cutoff logs a dose as "missed" if it's not confirmed within 90 minutes. Given you mentioned being out at the marina by then most days, would you like me to extend that window to 3 hours so your adherence log doesn't show false misses on busy mornings?

Also -- just a routine check since we haven't heard from you in a bit -- are you noticing any grogginess or dizziness with the current timing, or is it more just a logistics/scheduling issue on your end? No wrong answer, just want the notes accurate on our side.

Let me know and I'll finalize the settings.

Best,
Siobhan

Siobhan,

No grogginess or anything like that, it's purely a timing thing -- I'm just moving too fast in the mornings to babysit an app. Go ahead and extend that window to 3 hours, that'll cover me even on the days I'm out checking gear before sunrise.

6:00 with the 6:30 backup sounds perfect. Appreciate you actually asking instead of just guessing -- makes a difference.

Thanks, hon. That'll do it for now.

Jack

Siobhan,

Following up on the email I sent yesterday morning -- the one I banged out on my phone standing in the driveway with one boot on, headed out the door to the marina. Wanted to make sure it actually went through, because I never got a bounce-back or a confirmation number.

Short version: the bilge pump float switch I ordered off your site (order #48212, if that helps) showed up dead on arrival. No hum, no click, nothing, and I found that out at 5am checking the boat before pushing off, which is not when a man wants to be troubleshooting electrical.

I run the Bay Runner solo most days out of Annapolis, so a dead bilge switch isn't a "get to it next week" problem for me. Can you get a replacement shipped overnight, or point me to a marina supplier nearby who'd have one on the shelf? Full day of pots tomorrow, and Saturday's blocked off for my son Connor's game, so Friday's really my only window to sort this.

Appreciate you looking into it.

Jack Singleton
F/V Bay Runner

Mr. Singleton,

Thanks for following up, and sorry for the trouble -- I can confirm your original email did come through yesterday at 6:14 AM, it just got stuck in a queue behind a system update on our end, not on you. I have order #48212 pulled up now.

The float switch model (Rule-A500) has had a couple of DOA reports this month, so this isn't anything you did wrong. I can get a replacement out via overnight shipping at no charge -- it'll go out today and land by tomorrow afternoon. I'll also send a prepaid return label for the dead unit.

If you need something sooner, West Marine on Forest Drive in Annapolis usually stocks the Rule switches -- I'd call ahead to be safe. Can you confirm the shipping address on file is still correct, or is there a marina address you'd rather I use?

Best,
Siobhan
Customer Support

Siobhan,

Ship it to the house, same address on file -- Bridget's usually home to grab packages, and if not it'll sit fine on the porch, we're not exactly high-crime around here. Called West Marine like you said just in case, and they had one switch left, so I grabbed it and I'm already back up and running for tomorrow's pots. Keep the overnight one coming anyway as a spare -- better to have it than not, this boat doesn't forgive you twice in one season.

Appreciate you actually reading the email instead of sending me a form letter. Half the places I deal with, you're talking to a wall. Tide's dropping early tomorrow so I want to be off the dock by 5, and now I don't have to worry about the bilge doing something stupid while I'm out there alone.

Thanks again, hon -- genuinely.

Jack

Jack,

Glad West Marine came through, and glad to hear the Bay Runner's back in business for tomorrow. Your spare switch shipped about twenty minutes ago -- tracking number 1Z8847X2 -- set for delivery by end of day tomorrow, signature not required, so it'll be fine on the porch if Bridget's out.

I've flagged your account with a note about the Rule-A500 issue in case you ever need to reference this again, and refunded the shipping on your original order for the trouble. No need to return the dead unit -- toss it or keep it for parts.

Hope the pots are heavy tomorrow, and good luck to Connor at his game Saturday. Reach out anytime if something else comes up before you're headed out the door.

Take care,
Siobhan
Customer Support

Siobhan,

Follow up on that afternoon Gabapentin dose we talked about. Been taking the 300mg around 2pm like the doc said, but that's smack in the middle of hauling pots or running charters back in. Had a spell yesterday out past Thomas Point where I felt foggy at the wheel for about twenty minutes. Nothing crazy, but I don't love feeling anything but sharp out there with paying customers aboard.

Can we look at shifting that dose earlier, like right after I get back to the dock around noon, instead of mid-afternoon? Or is there a reason it's set up the way it is. I've got charters booked most of this month and Connor's got a game Saturday I'm not missing, so I need to know I can trust my head out on the water.

Let me know what you need from me - can call the office if easier.

Jack

Hi Jack,

Thanks for flagging this, and good instinct not to just push through it - dizziness at the wheel is exactly what we want to catch early. The mid-afternoon timing was set to keep your three doses evenly spaced through the day, but there's flexibility here. Shifting the afternoon 300mg to right after you're back at the dock around noon should still keep decent spacing from your morning dose with breakfast and your bedtime dose, so that's a reasonable adjustment.

A couple things: give it a few days at the new time and keep a quick note of how you feel out on the water, especially during any pot hauling or tight maneuvering. If the fogginess happens again even at the new time, or shows up with the morning dose too, that's worth a call rather than another email - we don't want you guessing out past Thomas Point.

Go ahead and make the switch starting tomorrow. I'll note the schedule change on your file so it's consistent going forward.

Hope Connor's game goes well Saturday.

Siobhan

Hey Siobhan,

Following up on ticket #4471 from last week -- the DocuSign API integration for our charter waivers is still throwing errors. Every time a client tries to sign the liability waiver from their phone, the envelope status just sits on "sent" and never flips to "completed" on our end, so my deckhand has no way to confirm before we shove off. Had two charters this week I basically had to double-check by phone, which defeats the whole point.

My son Patrick set up the original webhook endpoint for us back in the spring and swears the config hasn't changed, so I'm guessing something shifted on your end. Can someone take a look at the webhook logs for account ID BAYRUNNER-2291? I've got six charters booked out of Annapolis this weekend and would like this sorted before Saturday morning.

Appreciate the help.

Jack Singleton
F/V Bay Runner

Hi Jack,

Thanks for the ticket number, that helped me track it down fast. I pulled the logs for BAYRUNNER-2291 and you're right -- our webhook delivery service had a routing issue that started last Tuesday, which lines up with when you started seeing envelopes stuck on "sent." It wasn't anything on Patrick's end; the fix is on us.

I've reset the webhook subscription and pushed a retry on the failed notifications, so any waivers signed in the last 5 days should now show as completed in your dashboard -- worth having your deckhand refresh and check. I'd also recommend regenerating your API key as a precaution since the old one was flagged in the same incident; I can walk you or Patrick through that in under five minutes if you want to hop on a call.

Let me know if the completed statuses are showing up correctly on your end before the weekend. Sorry for the scramble this caused with your charters.

Best,
Siobhan

Siobhan,

Just checked -- statuses are flipping to completed now, dashboard looks clean. Patrick regenerated the API key this morning and tested it with a dummy waiver, came through fine. No need for the call, we're good.

Really appreciate the quick turnaround on this, especially with six charters on the books this weekend and my youngest's got a ballgame Saturday I'm not trying to miss either. Good to have it squared away.

Thanks again,
Jack

Hi Siobhan,

Following up on the ticket I filed last week (#48213, I think) about the Calendly API webhook not firing when a charter reschedules on the site. It's still happening -- had two parties show up Tuesday at 0600 for the same slot because the cancellation from the first booking never pushed through to our dispatch board here at the marina. Made for a hell of an awkward conversation on the dock before we even cast off.

Setup hasn't changed on our end since March -- same booking widget, same API key, synced to the shared calendar Bridget and I use to run the Bay Runner's schedule. Can you check if something shifted server-side, or if webhook deliveries are backing up in a queue somewhere?

Would like this sorted before the weekend. I've got charters booked out of Annapolis both days and no interest in double-booking six anglers on a 32-foot boat.

Thanks,
Jack Singleton
F/V Bay Runner

Hi Jack,

Thanks for the follow-up, and sorry you're still seeing this. I pulled up ticket #48213 and cross-referenced it with your account. Before I dig into the webhook logs, can you confirm a couple things: what API key prefix you're using (last 4 characters is fine, don't send the whole thing), and whether your dispatch board is set to Eastern Time or UTC? A number of accounts on the old webhook version have been seeing dropped events when there's a timezone mismatch between the Calendly account settings and the receiving endpoint.

Also -- do you have any error responses logged on your end when the webhook fails to fire? Even a timestamp range would help me trace it in our delivery logs.

I want to get you a real fix, not a band-aid, especially with charters going out both weekend days. I'll keep this ticket open and prioritized until we nail it down.

Best,
Siobhan
Calendly Support

Siobhan,

Appreciate you jumping on it. Key ends in 7F2A. Dispatch board's set to Eastern -- same as everything else I run, tides don't wait for UTC and neither do I.

No error codes on our side, just silence -- the reschedule goes through fine on the customer's end (I can see it in my Calendly dashboard), but the webhook to our board just never lands. Best I can tell it's happened three times in the past two weeks, all in the early morning window, roughly 5:30 to 7 AM. Tuesday's was around 5:45 AM.

Honestly at this point I've got Bridget manually checking the dashboard against the board every morning before I head out, which defeats the whole point of paying for the integration. Would rather not keep doing that -- she's got enough going on getting the kids out the door, and I need to be checking gear and the marine forecast, not staring at a screen.

Let me know what you find.

Jack

Hi Jack,

Good news -- found it. Your account was still on our legacy webhook delivery path, which has a known bug where events created in a short burst (like an early-morning reschedule right after a cancellation) can get silently dropped if the receiving endpoint doesn't respond within 2 seconds. Given your dispatch board is checking in during that same 5:30-7 AM stretch, that lines up exactly with what you described.

I've migrated your account to the newer webhook subscription with automatic retry logic (up to 3 attempts over 10 minutes), and confirmed your API key (ending 7F2A) is correctly scoped for it. I also double-checked your timezone setting -- Eastern is correctly applied on our end, so that wasn't the culprit, but good to rule out.

I ran two test reschedules against your endpoint just now and both came through clean on the first attempt. I'd like you to keep an eye on it through this weekend's charters as a real-world check, but you and Bridget should be able to stop the manual cross-check starting tomorrow.

I'll keep the ticket open through Monday in case anything slips through. Good luck out there this weekend.

Siobhan
Calendly Support

Hi Siobhan,

Following up on the ticket I opened last week (#48213) about the Typeform API webhook dropping charter booking submissions. Still happening. Had two bookings come through the site Friday and Saturday and neither one made it to my Google Sheet - only found out because the folks showed up at the dock in Annapolis and I had no idea they were coming. Not the kind of surprise I want on a charter morning.

I double-checked the API key against your docs, it matches. Endpoint URL is right too, checked it three times. Nothing's changed on my end since your last reply.

Can somebody actually pull the webhook logs on your side instead of sending me the same troubleshooting article again? I run this boat solo most days, I don't have time to keep chasing a form.

Thanks,
Jack Singleton
F/V Bay Runner

Hi Jack,

Thanks for the follow-up, and I'm sorry about the missed bookings - that's on us. I pulled the logs on ticket #48213 and found it: your webhook's signing secret was rotated during a platform update two weeks ago, but the notification email bounced, so your integration kept using the old secret. Every submission was failing signature verification silently on our end instead of hitting your sheet.

I've reissued a fresh secret and updated it directly on your account, so you shouldn't need to touch anything in the Typeform dashboard. I also ran three test submissions through your charter booking form just now and confirmed all three landed in the connected Google Sheet within a few seconds.

Could you have someone submit one real test booking from the live site in the next day or two, just to be safe before your next charter goes out? I'll keep an eye on the webhook logs from here and follow up Thursday either way.

Really sorry for the runaround - appreciate you sticking with it.

Best,
Siobhan

Hi Siobhan,

Following up on ticket #48213 from last week -- the webhook for the deposit payments still isn't firing right on the booking site. Had two charters book the same Saturday morning slot again yesterday because the API never marked the first one as paid. That's the third time this month and it's costing me actual trips, not just a headache.

I'm running the Square API tied into my charter booking page for the Bay Runner -- card reader on the dock works fine, it's just the online deposit hook that's dropping. Can someone actually look at the webhook logs on your end instead of just resending me the setup doc? I've already got that memorized at this point.

Appreciate you staying on it. Season's cranking up now so I need this fixed before next weekend, not after.

Jack Singleton
F/V Bay Runner

Hi Jack,

Thanks for the follow-up, and sorry this is still causing trouble -- I can see ticket #48213 and the two prior reports tied to your account. I pulled the webhook delivery logs on our end for the last 30 days and you're right, there's a pattern of failed deliveries specifically on the checkout.session.completed event, not a config issue on your booking page.

Could you confirm the exact date/time (with time zone) of yesterday's double-booking and the last 4 digits of the customer's card or the order ID if you have it? That'll let me match it against the specific delivery attempt and get it in front of our platform team as a bug rather than a setup question. I'm also going to flag your account for priority handling given the season timing.

I'll follow up within 24 hours once I have the delivery attempt pulled up. Thanks for your patience, Jack.

Best,
Siobhan
Square Support

Siobhan,

That's more like it -- appreciate someone actually digging into the logs. Double booking was yesterday, June 24th, around 9:15am Eastern. Don't have the last four of the card but the order ID off my dashboard is SQ-88214. Second booking that got double-slotted came in about ten minutes later.

One more thing while I've got you -- if this is a known bug on your end, I don't want to hear "should be fixed" and then get burned again in July when I've got back-to-back weekend charters booked solid. Let me know when it's actually verified fixed, not just patched.

Thanks for moving fast on this one.

Jack

Hey Siobhan,

Following up on the ticket I put in Tuesday about the morning marine forecast module -- the tide predictions for Chesapeake Bay Central are still coming up wrong. I run the Bay Runner out of Annapolis and I'm on the water by 0430 most mornings, so if the app says low tide at Thomas Point is off by an hour, that's a real problem when I'm setting pots near the flats.

I checked it again this morning against NOAA's actual numbers and there's about a 50-minute gap. Same issue as last week, so whatever patch went out didn't take. Can someone actually look at this instead of just closing the ticket?

Appreciate it,
Jack Singleton

Hi Jack,

Thanks for following up, and sorry for the runaround -- I can see your ticket from Tuesday and I agree it shouldn't have been closed without a fix confirmed. I'm going to reopen it and flag it as a data-sync issue rather than a general bug so it gets to the right team faster.

A couple things that'll help me pin it down: which station are you pulling the forecast from in the app settings (Thomas Point Light, or a different central Bay station), and are you on iOS or Android? Also, if you can grab a screenshot next time you notice the gap, that's gold for our engineers -- timestamp matters.

I'll keep you posted as soon as I hear back from the data team.

Best,
Siobhan

Siobhan,

Station is Thomas Point Light, iPhone, app's up to date as of this morning. I'll grab a screenshot tomorrow at first light and send it over.

Honestly this needs to get sorted before the weekend -- I've got Connor's game Saturday afternoon and I'm trying to plan my haul-out around the tide so I'm not still out there when it starts. If the tide window's wrong I either cut the day short for nothing or push my luck and run late.

Let me know what the engineers say.

Jack

Hi Jack,

Good news -- the data team found it. There was a stale feed from one of the NOAA relay stations that was overriding the live data for the central Bay zone, Thomas Point included. It's been corrected and pushed out; the tide times should match NOAA exactly now. Can you double check tomorrow morning and confirm on your end?

I also went ahead and credited your account for the next billing cycle for the trouble -- figured after two weeks of bad data that was fair.

Hope you get out and back in plenty of time for Connor's game Saturday. Let me know if anything still looks off.

Best,
Siobhan

Morning Siobhan,

Quick one before I head out to the marina. Been on the Losartan 50 and Gabapentin 300 with breakfast for a few weeks now, same as we set up, but some mornings I'm barely eating - just coffee and out the door if the tide and forecast look right for pulling pots early. Does it screw anything up taking them with just coffee and a granola bar instead of a real breakfast? Don't want the Losartan messing with my blood pressure if I'm not eating enough, and the gabapentin's been fine for the knee and the back but I don't want to mess with a good thing.

Also wanted to check the afternoon and bedtime gabapentin doses are still 300 each, same as before - three times a day total, right? Been so busy with Connor's ballgames and the boat I want to make sure I'm not doubling up or missing something.

Thanks hon,
Jack

Good morning Jack,

Good question, and no, a light breakfast like coffee and a granola bar is enough to take the Losartan 50mg and Gabapentin 300mg safely - you don't need a full meal, just something in your stomach so the gabapentin doesn't cause nausea. The main thing with Losartan is consistency with timing day to day, not the size of the meal, so keeping it with your morning routine before you head to the marina is exactly right.

And yes, you've got it correct: Gabapentin 300mg three times daily - morning with breakfast, afternoon, and again at bedtime. Total of 900mg a day across the three doses. If you ever have a morning where you're rushing out early for the tide and skip food entirely, that's the one time to at least grab something small first, even a few crackers.

Glad the combo's been steady for you. Let me know if you notice any dizziness on the days you eat lighter, and we can look at timing again.

Take care,
Siobhan

Morning hon,

Heading out the door to the marina now, tide's turning around 9 and forecast looks decent for once -- wind out of the southwest under 10 knots, so we should get pots pulled clean without everything blowing sideways. Wanted to follow up on Saturday before I lose cell signal out on the water -- can you confirm you're good to grab Connor for his game? I've got a charter booked that morning and your mother's got her hands full with Grandma Kathleen's doctor run.

Also, did Tommy ever get back to you on the pot order? I need to know before I call the marine supply place, don't want to double up on wire.

Love you, talk later,
Dad

Hi Dad,

Got your email -- yes, I've got Connor covered Saturday, I'll swing by the house around 8:30 and get him to the field by 9. Don't worry about it, go pull your pots.

Tommy texted me back last night, said he can do the order but wants to know if you're going with the same count as last spring or bumping it up. He said to just call the shop directly since he's out on his own boat most of today. I told him you'd probably reach out this afternoon once you're back in.

Also -- Mom mentioned you were moving slow this morning getting out the door. Everything okay? You don't have to get into it over email, just checking in. Love you, be safe out there.

Siobhan

Hey hon,

Thanks for covering Connor, that's a load off. And yeah I called Tommy on the way to the boat, told him same count as last spring plus twenty more, water's been good to us and I'd rather have extra pots than come up short in August.

I'm fine, just an old body on a boat, nothing new. Stiff in the mornings same as always, your mother worries more than she needs to. Had breakfast, took care of everything I'm supposed to, and I'm out on the water now so don't fret.

Tell Connor good luck Saturday, I'll try to get back in time but no promises with this tide schedule. Love you, kiddo.

Dad

Siobhan,

Quick one for you. That afternoon reminder for the Gabapentin 300mg keeps firing at 1pm and half the time I'm out past the Bay Bridge with a charter and don't feel the phone buzz in my slicker pocket. By the time I'm back at the dock it's 4:30 and I've missed the window twice this week.

Can you push that afternoon alert to something later, like 2:30 or 3? Most of my trips are booked noon to five this time of year, crabs are running good so I'm not walking away from the pots to check a phone. Let me know what you need from me to get it changed.

Thanks, hon.

Jack

Good morning Jack,

Thanks for flagging this -- happy to get it sorted so it actually fits your schedule instead of the other way around. Before I adjust anything, I want to make sure we keep proper spacing from your morning dose so it lines up with what Dr. Reyes has on file.

A few things: what time do you typically take the AM dose with breakfast, and roughly what time do most charters wrap for the day? Also, are you still doing the bedtime dose without issues, or has the schedule shift been causing any gaps there too?

Once I have that I can move the afternoon reminder and update it on our end so it's consistent, not just a one-off fix.

Best,
Siobhan

Siobhan,

AM dose is right around 6, 6:15 with the Losartan and coffee before I head to the marina. Charters usually wrap 5, sometimes later if the fish are biting or somebody's kid catches their first rockfish and wants ten more pictures. Bedtime dose is fine, no issues there, that one I never miss because I'm dead on my feet by 9:30 anyway.

2:30 would work good -- that's usually when I'm running back in or already tied up doing paperwork. If it's easier just set it for 2:30 every day instead of trying to chase the charter schedule, that's fine by me. Simpler is better, I've got enough moving parts between the boat and Connor's ballgames.

Appreciate you handling this.

Jack

Hi Jack,

That all sounds good and keeps solid spacing between doses. I've updated the afternoon Gabapentin 300mg reminder to a fixed 2:30pm daily instead of tying it to variable timing -- one less thing for you to think about when you're on the water.

I also flagged the account so if you ever need to shift it seasonally (like once things slow down after crab season), just send me a note and I'll adjust it, no need to go through the whole intake process again. Everything else on file -- morning and bedtime doses -- stays as is.

Glad we got this squared away. Give Connor a good luck for me at his next game.

Take care,
Siobhan
